title: Kelbis Nu
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Youmay|the Jedi Knight and Jensaarai Kelbis Nu|the New Republic pilot [[Koobis Nu]]|linebreak=1}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Njo_kubisnu.svg]] |name=Kelbis Nu |homeworld=[[Susevfi]]''[[I, Jedi]]'' |birth= |death=[[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]'' |species=[[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Black]]{{InsiderCite|57|Who's Who in the New Jedi Order}} |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] *[[New Jedi Order]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] |masters=[[Unidentified female Saarai-kaar|Saarai-kaar]] |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Ya-ya—ya…Yag'Dhul.|Kelbis Nu's final words|}} '''Kelbis Nu''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who grew up on [[Susevfi]] as part of the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] community. The Jensaarai were in enforced service to former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Leonia Tavira]], who threatened to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the population of Susevfi if the Jensaarai did not serve her. Kelbis Nu was one of five Jensaarai that were sent to [[Courkrus]] to [[Duel on Courkrus|ambush]] the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]]. They lost the encounter when [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] arrived to help Horn, and Nu was knocked unconscious. Horn and Skywalker subsequently defeated the leader of the Jensaarai, and forced Tavira to leave Susevfi. Nu later became a member of the [[New Jedi Order]]. He fought against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], and was later [[Skirmish on Eriadu (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attacked]] and [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the Yuuzhan Vong-affiliated [[Peace Brigade]] on [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]]. ==Biography== ===Jensaarai=== {{Quote|You did what you felt was right to save others from being hurt.|Luke Skywalker comments on the actions of the Jensaarai|}} The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] Kelbis Nu grew up on [[Susevfi]] as part of a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] sect, the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]]. The Jensaarai were led by a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Unidentified female Saarai-kaar|Saarai-kaar]] who kept the movement growing. Under her leadership,{{PolyhedronCite|157|I, Jensaarai}} Nu constructed his own orange-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and a suit of [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Jensaarai armor|armor]]; the latter considered the final test in a Jensaarai's training. Nu was promoted to the rank of Defender, and worked with four other Jensaarai Defenders stationed on ex-[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Leonia Tavira]]'s [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], the ''[[Invidious]]'', detecting potential traps set for the woman. Meanwhile, Tavira held the Susevfi population hostage in order to ensure cooperation from the Jensaarai. [[File:Saarai-Kaarsm.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The Saarai-Kaar; Nu's leader while a Jensaarai]] While Nu was serving Tavira, she kidnapped [[Mirax Terrik Horn|the wife]] of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]]. In order to track down his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], Horn infiltrated one of Tavira's [[Invids|pirate]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]], the [[Khuiumin Survivors]]. He then haunted the base, disguising himself as a Jedi Knight. Tavira dispatched Nu and four other Jensaarai to apprehend Horn. They tracked him down on [[Courkrus]] and trapped him in an alley, unaffected by the [[Mind trick/Legends#Force illustion|Force illusions]] he conjured against them. Just as Kelbis and the other Jensaarai were about to attack Horn, however, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] arrived—guided by the [[Gand/Legends|Gand]] [[Ooryl Qrygg]]—and helped Horn fight the Jensaarai. While three of the Jensaarai [[Duel on Courkrus|attacked]] Skywalker, Nu and another fought against Horn. Horn quickly defeated them both, catching Nu by the wrist and knocking him out with a blow from the end of his lightsaber [[Lightsaber hilt/Legends|pommel]] to the right side of his head. Using a [[Ysalamiri/Legends|ysalamir]] to confuse the Jensaari, as well as the presence of Skywalker as intimidation, Horn interrogated Nu and the other Jensaarai until they revealed their origins and the location of the Jensaarai base. While Skywalker and Horn traveled to Susevfi to defeat Tavira and rescue Horn's wife, the five Defenders, including Nu, were kept on Courkrus in the [[Prison/Legends|custody]] of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Jacob Nive]], remaining under the [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-neutralizing presence of a ysalamir. Horn and Skywalker went on to defeat the Jensaarai and convince their leader, the [[Saarai-kaar]], that the Jedi were not evil as they had believed. Skywalker then extended an invitation for the Jensaarai to join the Jedi and train at the [[Jedi Praxeum|Praxeum]]. In due course, Kelbis Nu took up this offer and became a Jedi Knight in the [[New Jedi Order]]. ===Jedi Knight=== {{Quote|He's gone.''"
"''He can't be. I was going to save him.|Anakin Solo and Tahiri Veila on Nu's death|}} As a Jedi Knight, Nu took an active role in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. It was while monitoring [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong fleet|fleet]] movements on a trip to [[Yag-prime|Yag'Dhul Station]] that he discovered the undercover Yuuzhan Vong [[Executor (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|agent]] [[Nom Anor]]. Fleeing, he was caught on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] by members of the Yuuzhan Vong-affiliated [[Peace Brigade]]. Nu was cornered in an alley by five Peace Brigade assailants; two armed with [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and three with [[Stun baton/Legends|stun batons]]. His former opponent and now fellow Jedi, Corran Horn, was also on Eriadu at the time, but detected Nu's presence too dimly in the Force to be able to help him. The young Jedi [[Anakin Solo]] and [[Tahiri Veila]], however, were much closer and came running to his aid. Still, one of the Peace Brigade members shot Nu before they could arrive. While Solo and Veila managed to [[Skirmish on Eriadu (Yuuzhan Vong War)|chase off]] the Peace Brigade thugs, they were too late to save Nu's life. Straining with his last breath, Kelbis managed to whisper to the young Jedi the Vong's next target—[[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]], the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the mathematical [[Givin/Legends|Givin]]. Though it [[Death/Legends|cost him his life]], the information was enough to enable a Givin and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] victory at the ensuing [[Battle of Yag'Dhul (26 ABY)|Battle of Yag'Dhul]]. ==Personality and traits== As a Jedi, Kelbis Nu took part in the Yuuzhan Vong War, during which he showed devotion to the safety of the New Republic by using his final breath to warn Solo and Veila of an impending Yuuzhan Vong attack on Yag-Dhul. ==Powers and abilities== As a member of first the Jensaarai and later the Jedi Order, Nu was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. Nu was skilled enough to become a Jensaarai Defender, and he was later deemed worthy of the rank of Jedi Knight by Luke Skywalker. His training as a Jensaarai enabled him to see through the Force illusions that Corran Horn once use against him on Courkrus. Nu was skilled in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] although he was unable to overcome a heavily armed, five-member Peace Brigade team sent to kill him. ==Behind the scenes== Kelbis Nu was created by [[Greg Keyes]] for a brief appearance in the novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]''. Later, [[Daniel Wallace]] provided some backstory for Nu in ''[[Who's Who in the New Jedi Order]]'', an article in [[Star Wars Insider 57|''Star Wars Insider'' 57]]. This identified Nu as a previously anonymous Rodian member of the Jensaarai, from [[Michael A. Stackpole]]'s ''[[I, Jedi]]'', although Wallace erroneously referred to Nu's first name as "Kubis" in his article. [[Dan Veesenmeyer]] and [[Russell Walks]] illustrated the ''Insider'' article, providing the only canonical depiction of the Jedi Knight. ==Appearances== *''[[I, Jedi]]'' {{Ab|I, Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} {{Ret}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]'' {{1stID|Kelbis Nu}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|57|Who's Who in the New Jedi Order}} *''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kelbis Nu |es=Kelbis Nu |esGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nu, Kelbis}} [[Category:Gray Jedi]] [[Category:Jedi Knights of the New Jedi Order]] [[Category:Jensaarai]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:New Republic individuals]] [[Category:Rodians]]
